/cwe/weakness/337.Name: Predictable Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
/cwe/weakness/337.Description: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) is initialized from a predictable seed, such as the process ID or system time.
/cwe/weakness/337.ExtendedDescription: The use of predictable seeds significantly reduces the number of possible seeds that an attacker would need to test in order to predict which random numbers will be generated by the PRNG.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: REALIZATION: This weakness is caused during implementation of an architectural security tactic.

Entries.IntroText: Both of these examples use a statistical PRNG seeded with the current value of the system clock to generate a random number:

Entries.BodyText: An attacker can easily predict the seed used by these PRNGs, and so also predict the stream of random numbers generated. Note these examples also exhibit CWE-338 (Use of Cryptographically Weak PRNG).

PotentialMitigations.Description: Use non-predictable inputs for seed generation.
Use products or modules that conform to FIPS 140-2 [REF-267] to avoid obvious entropy problems, or use the more recent FIPS 140-3 [REF-1192] if possible.

Use a PRNG that periodically re-seeds itself using input from high-quality sources, such as hardware devices with high entropy. However, do not re-seed too frequently, or else the entropy source might block.
